---
title: "Process"
---

The `.process` style renders items as a linear flow of nodes connected by arrows — left to right by default, or top to bottom with the `.vertical` modifier. Like all styles in this extension, it is activated via `filters: [diagrams]`.

## Basic usage

````markdown
::: process
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
:::
````

::: process
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
:::

## Vertical orientation

Add the `.vertical` modifier to flow top-to-bottom.

````markdown
::: {.process .vertical}
::: item
Input
:::
::: item
Process
:::
::: item
Output
:::
:::
````

::: {.process .vertical}
::: item
Input
:::
::: item
Process
:::
::: item
Output
:::
:::

## Arrow types

The default is `arrow-chevron`. Use `arrow-thin` or `arrow-double` for line-based arrows.

````markdown
::: {.process .arrow-thin}
::: item
Plan
:::
::: item
Build
:::
::: item
Ship
:::
:::
````

::: {.process .arrow-thin}
::: item
Plan
:::
::: item
Build
:::
::: item
Ship
:::
:::

## Chevron style

Add the `.chevron` modifier to render each step as an interlocking arrow tile, with no separate connectors. It works with `.vertical`, `.gap`, and the color system.

````markdown
::: {.process .chevron}
::: item
Plan
:::
::: item
Build
:::
::: item
Ship
:::
::: item
Measure
:::
:::
````

::: {.process .chevron}
::: item
Plan
:::
::: item
Build
:::
::: item
Ship
:::
::: item
Measure
:::
:::

It also flows top-to-bottom with `.vertical`:

::: {.process .chevron .vertical node-color="#7a3b5c"}
::: item
Plan
:::
::: item
Build
:::
::: item
Ship
:::
:::

## Arrow style

The `.arrow` modifier is a chevron sub-variant with deeper points and shorter, more landscape tiles, so each step reads as a distinct arrowhead rather than a block with a thin seam. It supports the same options as `.chevron` (`.vertical`, `.gap`, colors, annotations).

````markdown
::: {.process .arrow}
::: item
Plan
:::
::: item
Build
:::
::: item
Ship
:::
::: item
Measure
:::
:::
````

::: {.process .arrow}
::: item
Plan
:::
::: item
Build
:::
::: item
Ship
:::
::: item
Measure
:::
:::

It pairs well with a per-item color ramp:

::: {.process .arrow}
::: {.item color="#f0a830"}
Saturn
:::
::: {.item color="#f08a5d"}
Jupiter
:::
::: {.item color="#ee5a8a"}
Venus
:::
::: {.item color="#d44ad4"}
Mercury
:::
::: {.item color="#9b2fae"}
Mars
:::
:::

## Node shapes

The default is `node-box`. Use `node-circle` or `node-none` for alternatives.

````markdown
::: {.process .node-circle}
::: item
Start
:::
::: item
Middle
:::
::: item
End
:::
:::
````

::: {.process .node-circle}
::: item
Start
:::
::: item
Middle
:::
::: item
End
:::
:::

## Extra spacing with `.gap`

Add `.gap` to an item to enlarge the gap *before* it — useful for visually separating stages.

````markdown
::: process
::: item
A
:::
::: item
B
:::
::: {.item .gap}
C
:::
::: item
D
:::
:::
````

::: process
::: item
A
:::
::: item
B
:::
::: {.item .gap}
C
:::
::: item
D
:::
:::

## Colors

Use `node-color=` / `arrow-color=` on the container, or `color=` on individual items.

````markdown
::: {.process node-color="#7a3b5c" arrow-color="#444"}
::: item
Plan
:::
::: {.item color="#3b7a5c"}
Build
:::
::: item
Ship
:::
:::
````

::: {.process node-color="#7a3b5c" arrow-color="#444"}
::: item
Plan
:::
::: {.item color="#3b7a5c"}
Build
:::
::: item
Ship
:::
:::

## Annotations

Give any item a nested `.annotation` div to attach an external callout that
points at its step with a leader line. Callouts alternate above/below a
horizontal flow (or left/right of a `.vertical` flow). The annotation content is
free-form Markdown; any bold text or heading is tinted to match the step color
(optional). Annotate all, some, or none of the steps.

Nest the fenced divs with **more colons on the outer fences** (`:::::` for the
chart, `::::` for each item, `:::` for the annotation) and leave a blank line
around the inner fences.

````markdown
::::: process

:::: item
Draft

::: annotation
**Draft** write the first version of the content
:::

::::

:::: item
Review

::: annotation
**Review** gather feedback from the team
:::

::::

:::: item
Revise

::: annotation
**Revise** apply the suggested changes
:::

::::

:::: item
Publish

::: annotation
**Publish** release the finished content
:::

::::

:::::
````

::::: process

:::: item
Draft

::: annotation
**Draft** write the first version of the content
:::

::::

:::: item
Review

::: annotation
**Review** gather feedback from the team
:::

::::

:::: item
Revise

::: annotation
**Revise** apply the suggested changes
:::

::::

:::: item
Publish

::: annotation
**Publish** release the finished content
:::

::::

:::::

A `.vertical` process places the callouts to the left and right instead.

::::: {.process .vertical}

:::: item
Apply

::: annotation
**Apply** submit your application online
:::

::::

:::: item
Interview

::: annotation
**Interview** meet with the hiring team
:::

::::

:::: item
Offer

::: annotation
**Offer** receive and accept the offer
:::

::::

:::::

Annotations work with the `.chevron` subtype too — the callouts attach to the
interlocking arrow tiles.

````markdown
::::: {.process .chevron}

:::: item
Draft

::: annotation
**Draft** write the first version of the content
:::

::::

:::: item
Review

::: annotation
**Review** gather feedback from the team
:::

::::

:::: item
Revise

::: annotation
**Revise** apply the suggested changes
:::

::::

:::: item
Publish

::: annotation
**Publish** release the finished content
:::

::::

:::::
````

::::: {.process .chevron}

:::: item
Draft

::: annotation
**Draft** write the first version of the content
:::

::::

:::: item
Review

::: annotation
**Review** gather feedback from the team
:::

::::

:::: item
Revise

::: annotation
**Revise** apply the suggested changes
:::

::::

:::: item
Publish

::: annotation
**Publish** release the finished content
:::

::::

:::::

The `.arrow` subtype carries annotations the same way:

````markdown
::::: {.process .arrow}

:::: item
Draft

::: annotation
**Draft** write the first version of the content
:::

::::

:::: item
Review

::: annotation
**Review** gather feedback from the team
:::

::::

:::: item
Revise

::: annotation
**Revise** apply the suggested changes
:::

::::

:::: item
Publish

::: annotation
**Publish** release the finished content
:::

::::

:::::
````

::::: {.process .arrow}

:::: item
Draft

::: annotation
**Draft** write the first version of the content
:::

::::

:::: item
Review

::: annotation
**Review** gather feedback from the team
:::

::::

:::: item
Revise

::: annotation
**Revise** apply the suggested changes
:::

::::

:::: item
Publish

::: annotation
**Publish** release the finished content
:::

::::

:::::

The leader lines can be restyled (dashed, dotted, removed) and the colors
adjusted — see the [Annotations](annotations.qmd) concept page for the full set
of `ann-line`, `ann-line-color`, and `ann-title-color` options, which work on
every annotated layout.
